teamObjective.explanation: The 3 is the safest and most conventional opening lead: it minimizes future liability, provides no exploitable high-card signal, and aligns with the likely need to let partner or an opponent win the trick.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner has 3 unknown cards and no public signals. They may be holding mid-to-high cards that can cover a low lead, but this is only probabilistic; I cannot assume specific ranks or suits.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents each hold 3 unknown cards. With trump rank 7 and no trumps in my hand, any opponent with a 7 or higher (very likely) can beat any of my low spades.

actionRationale.primaryReason: The 3 is the safest and most conventional opening lead: it minimizes future liability, provides no exploitable high-card signal, and aligns with the likely need to let partner or an opponent win the trick.
